title: Task-pack template + canonical pack model
context: Every open-map-gaps task pack must be structured consistently so that reviewers, validators, and human mappers can quickly find the area-of-interest, authorized imagery, source datasets, tagging schema, mapping instructions, do-no-harm screen decision, and contribution channel. The canonical pack model (defined in PLAN.md) specifies the required fields: id, region, imagery, sources, features, doNoHarm, channel, organisedEditingWikiRef, attribution, acceptanceCriteria, and outcome. This template is a prerequisite for every subsequent pack task.
objective: Author and commit the canonical task-pack template and model so that all subsequent packs (pilot-007 onward) share a consistent, reviewable structure.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed task-pack template document covers all canonical model fields: id, region (name/aoiGeoJsonRef/adminLevel/partner/useStatement/localConsent), imagery (allowed layers with provider/layerUrl/osmPermissionRef/attribution), sources (name/url/license/snapshotRef), features (key/tagSchemaRef/mappingNotes/validationRules), doNoHarm, channel, organisedEditingWikiRef, attribution, acceptanceCriteria, and outcome.
Template includes a completed example section (with placeholder values) so a new pack author knows exactly what to fill in.
Deliverable-enum mapping is documented: pack/guide/playbook → document; candidate-feature set/MapRoulette challenge → dataset (ODbL-1.0); tooling → pr; translated instructions → translation.
Template records that no OSM write credentials are used and no AI-generated geometry is uploaded directly.
Template is reviewed by the technical reviewer and referenced by tagging-005 and subsequent packs.
resources: HOT Tasking Manager project template — https://tasks.hotosm.org
output: A committed task-pack template document and canonical model definition, ready to be copied and filled in per area, with all required fields and a completed placeholder example.
